Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: religious visual works [300234093], visual works by function [300191089], visual works (works) [300191086], Visual Works (hierarchy name) [300179869], Visual and Verbal Communication (hierarchy name) [300264552], Objects Facet [300264092]

Non-Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: paintings by material or technique [300033701], paintings (visual works) [300033618], visual works by material or technique [300191091], visual works (works) [300191086], Visual Works (hierarchy name) [300179869], Visual and Verbal Communication (hierarchy name) [300264552], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Designs created by pouring colored sands, powdered pigments from minerals or crystals, or pigments from other natural or synthetic sources onto a surface to make a fixed or unfixed image. They are often made on the ground or the floor rather than on a separate support. Unfixed sand paintings are created or used in religious and healing rituals by various groups, including Navajo, Pueblo, and Zuni in North America, Australian Aborigines, Tibetans, Japanese, and Hindus in various nations. Sand paintings may be fixed, often with works of relatively small size for sale to the tourist trade.

Diseños ceremoniales altamente convencionalizados en un número limitado de colores, compuestos por arena y otros materiales naturales tales como minerales o polen. Estos son creadas o usadas en ritules religiosos por varios grupos incluyendo los Navajo, Pueblo y Zuni en Norteamérica, aborígenes australianos, tibetanos, japoneses e hindues.

Zeer gestileerde, ceremoniële ontwerpen in een beperkt aantal kleuren, samengesteld uit zand en andere natuurlijke materialen zoals mineralen of pollen. Ze worden gemaakt en gebruikt bij godsdienstige rituelen door verschillende bevolkingsgroepen, zoals de Navajo, Pueblo en Zuni in Noord-Amerika, de Aboriginals in Australië, en door Tibetanen, Japanners en Hindoes.
由彩沙、取自礦物或水晶的粉末顏料，或其他天然或合成顏料，灑至表面創作而成的固定或不固定圖像，通常不在獨立的基底材料上，而於地面或地板創作。北美印第安人如納瓦霍人、普埃布洛人、祖尼人，澳洲原住民，藏族，日本人及各國的印度教人士等常將不固定式的沙畫用於宗教或治療儀式之中。沙畫也有固定形式，通常為賣給觀光客的小型作品。

Darstellung, die durch Gießen von gefärbtem Sand, zermahlenen Pigmenten von Mineralien oder Kristallen oder Pigmenten von anderen natürlichen oder synthetischen Quellen auf einer Fläche angelegt ist, um ein festes oder loses Bild zu schaffen. Sie wird oft auf dem Boden erstellt anstatt auf einer separaten Unterlage. Lose Sandbilder werden in religiösen Ritualen oder Heilungsritualen durch verschiedene Gruppen geschaffen oder verwendet, einschließlich Navajos, Pueblo-Indianer und Zuni in Nordamerika, australische Aborigines, Tibeter, Japaner und Hindus in verschiedenen Nationen. Sandbilder können fixiert sein, häufig als Werk von geringer Größe für den Verkauf an Touristen.

Projetos criados despejando areias coloridas, pigmentos em pó de minerais ou cristais ou pigmentos de outras fontes naturais ou sintéticas em uma superfície para fazer uma imagem fixa ou não fixa. Frequentemente, são feitos no solo ou no chão, e não em um suporte separado. Pinturas de areia não fixadas são criadas ou usadas em rituais religiosos e de cura por vários grupos, incluindo navajo, pueblo e zuni na América do Norte, aborígenes australianos, tibetanos, japoneses e hindus em várias nações. Pinturas de areia podem ser consertadas, muitas vezes com obras de tamanho relativamente pequeno para venda ao comércio turístico.

Term_Source.Page: text search found one reference citation with this spelling
illustration, 500, caption to illustration and used in text

Source.Source_ID: 2000048873/Dewey, Sistema de Clasificación Decimal Dewey e Índice Relativo (2000)
